title: How We Find Planets We Can't See
script_summary: Exploring how we detect invisible exoplanets through their effects on stars.

segments.voiceover: Most exoplanets are invisible — we find them by watching stars wobble and dim.
Two main methods help us: the transit method and radial velocity.
The transit method tracks a star's light, looking for dimming as a planet crosses.
Meanwhile, radial velocity detects tiny wobbles in a star's position caused by a planet's gravity.
These methods have helped discover thousands of exoplanets, revealing a universe full of possibilities.
Curious about our universe? Like and follow for more mind-bending space facts!
